alternative_explanations: C_GIVEN_P has the largest absolute same-time Delta, but this may partly reflect inherited C-layer near-redundancy and same-day structure rather than a causal layer-forming mechanism.
V_GIVEN_PCT has higher Lift than T_GIVEN_PC at W250/q20 while having lower Delta; this is a base-rate effect because V's target marginal rate is lower.
V q10 pooled positive Delta differs from the security-level median sign, so pooled results can be driven by higher-weight or higher-anchor-count securities rather than a majority-security effect.

Strict-past percentile nonuniformity, availability differences, and rolling-window dependence can affect nominal q hit rates; the q nesting artifact verifies set nesting but does not replace later null models.
anomaly_review: Reviewed the committed anomaly scan and revised q nesting artifact. Mandatory checks are passed, blocking anomalies are empty, and nonblocking material warnings are retained for inherited R1-T05 issues plus V q10 pooled/security sign reversals. These warnings limit interpretation but do not block final-gate.
baseline_challenger_review: Reviewed the fixed 27-row grid without selecting a winner. At W250/q20, C_GIVEN_P, T_GIVEN_PC, and V_GIVEN_PCT all have nonzero denominators, anchors, targets, and child counts. Baseline Lift ranking is C > V > T, while Delta ranking is C > T > V, so the analysis correctly avoids ranking by Lift alone.

independent_recomputations.calculation: S_PC derived/R0 W250/q20 true=false=null counts match: 132775, 1370896, 227098
denominator key-set lower_not_in_higher + higher_not_in_lower across all q pairs

parameter_response_review: Reviewed step-specific denominators, all-four denominator sensitivity, q count monotonicity, row-level q nesting, denominator key-set equality, W availability monotonicity, nested three-valued reconciliation, and metric identities. The revised R1-T06-20260710T1216Z artifacts correctly distinguish legal high-q active-set expansion from denominator equality.
